revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح هارونا]][[en:Haruna Shrine]][[fr:Haruna-jinja]][[ja:榛名神社]] {{otheruses|The shrine in Harunasan-machi, Takasaki City, Gunma Prefecture|}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Haruna Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haruna Honden.JPG|270px]]
The shrine grounds | location = Harunasan-machi 849, {{ill|Takasaki|en|Takasaki|qid=Q336438|lt=Takasaki|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|27|31|N|138|51|8|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]]
[[Haniyasu-hime]] | sacred_object = | type = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha (minor)]]
Sixth shrine of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Traditional: [[Emperor Yōmei]], 1st year ([[586]]) | main_building = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] | alternate_name = Mangyō Gongen | annual_festival = [[May 15]] | rituals = See [[#Annual rituals]] | map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Haruna Shrine|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|27|31|N|138|51|8|E}}|frame-latitude=36.49|frame-longitude=138.91}} }} '''Haruna Shrine''' (榛名神社, *Haruna Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Harunasan-machi]], {{ill|Takasaki|en|Takasaki|qid=Q336438|lt=Takasaki|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}}, one of the six shrines of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and was Ranked as a [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Modern typeing System]].{{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=群馬県地域創生部文化財保護課 |title=群馬県近世寺社総合調査報告書－歴史的建造物を中心に－神社編 |origdate=2022-03-18 |date=2022-03-18 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/121895 |location=群馬県前橋市大手町一丁目１番１号 |ncid=BC14492681 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.121895 |publisher= |pages=129-142}} == Overview== Haruna Shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Mount Haruna|en|Mount Haruna|qid=Q1191690|lt=Mount Haruna|1=Mount Haruna}}, one of the [[Three Mountains of Jōmō]] along with {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} and {{ill|Mount Myōgi|en|Mount Myōgi|qid=Q994071|lt=Mount Myōgi|1=Mount Myōgi}}. The current enshrined deities are the fire deity [[Kagu-tsuchi|Kagutsuchi]] and the earth deity [[Haniyasu-hime]]. In addition, the shrine honors deities such as [[Mikumari no Kami|Mikumari]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Ōyamatsumi]], [[Ōmononushi]], and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Sakuya-hime]]. Archaeological evidence of ancient temple structures on the grounds suggests that the shrine has long been associated with [[Shinbutsu-shūgō]] (the syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism). In the medieval period, it was known as "Mangyō Gongen," and its associated Buddhist deity was [[:en:Jizō Bosatsu|Jizō Bosatsu]] (Kṣitigarbha). During the early modern period, the shrine operated as the temple Harunasan Gandenji, and a {{ill|Three-storied pagoda|ja|三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|2=ja|3=三重塔|4=zh|5=三重塔|1=Three-storied pagoda|lt=Three-storied pagodas|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|13=User:Immanuelle/Three-storied pagoda|qq=}} (now the Treasure Hall) still stands on the premises. In the early modern period, the shrine’s deities were arranged as follows: [[Haniyasu-hime]] at the upper level, [[:en:Kunitokotachi|Kunitokotachi]], [[Izanagi]], {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}, and [[Ōkuninushi]] at the middle level, and [[Nigihayahi]], [[Hikohohodemi]] at the lower level. However, the *Haruna San-shi* (*Chronicles of Mount Haruna*) describes different arrangements, with Nigihayahi in the east, Motoakari no Mikoto in the central hall, and Shizumamichi no Mikoto in the west hall. In {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}, the Meiji government consolidated the shrine’s pantheon to its current two principal deities. The area surrounding Haruna Shrine, including its forest, is protected as a designated Green Conservation Area of Gunma Prefecture.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.gunma.jp/page/7121.html|title=榛名神社|publisher=群馬県|date=|accessdate=2024-12-24}} == History == === Ancient period === According to the "Haruna Shrine Records," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|qid=Q313321|lt=Emperor Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}}, {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10949054}}'s son {{ill|Umashimade no Mikoto|ja|可美真手命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimade no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウマシマジ|1=Umashimaji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10946169}} and his father erected sacred fences (himorogi) in the mountains and worshipped {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天神地祇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미}}, beginning the shrine's history. It is said that the ritual place was formally established in the first year of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|qid=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}} (586){{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011b|p=257}}. The earliest documentary mention of Haruna Shrine is in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, completed in {{ill|Enchō|en|Enchō|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 (927), where it is listed as a minor shrine in {{ill|Gunma District, Gunma|en|Gunma District, Gunma|qid=Q5617131|lt=Gunma District|1=Gunma District, Gunma}} among the twelve shrines of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011a|p=417}}{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011b|p=257}}{{Sfn|群馬県教育委員会|1976|p=1}}. Mt. Haruna was called "Ikaho no Nero" in ancient times, and the Engishiki Jinmyōchō also lists {{ill|Ikaho Shrine|ja|伊香保神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikaho Shrine|1=Ikaho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17220841}}, indicating that both shrines worshipped Mt. Haruna from different locations{{Sfn|相葉|1962|p=113}}{{Sfn|群馬県教育委員会|1976|p=1}}. Scholars suggest that local clans, such as the Arima for Ikaho Shrine and Kurumamochi for Haruna Shrine, maintained these distinct worship traditions{{Sfn|群馬県教育委員会|1976|pp=1-2}}{{Sfn|相葉|1962|pp=127-128}}{{Sfn|群馬県教育委員会|1976|p=3}}. The current mountainous location is attributed to Buddhist, particularly esoteric Buddhist influences{{Sfn|相葉|1962|pp=127-128}}. The "Haruna Shrine Iwayama Ruins," believed to be monk quarters, date back to the 9th century{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011a|pp=666-670}}{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011b|p=257}}. In the {{ill|Chōgen|en|Chōgen|qid=Q1193557|lt=Jōgen|1=Chōgen}} era (1028-1037), documents show established shrine buildings{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011a|p=677}}{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011b|pp=257-258}}. === Medieval period === In 1190, Haruna Shrine had already become a temple called Haruna-ji due to Shinbutsu-shūgō (fusion of Shinto and Buddhism){{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011a|pp=418-419}}{{Sfn|関|1999|pp=36-37}}. Eight mirrors with images of Kannon demonstrate this fusion, dating from the 12th to the 13th centuries{{Sfn|関|1999|pp=37-38}}. During the Nanboku-chō period, Haruna Shrine appeared in the "Shintōshū," associated with Jizō Bosatsu{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011a|p=680}}{{Sfn|関|1999|pp=41-42}}. Monastic governance was led by abbots claiming descent from Fujiwara no Michinaga{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011a|pp=587-592}}. {{Main|Raiin}} The shrine saw control shift to warrior families in the Muromachi period{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2011a|p=592}}. === Early modern period === During the Edo period, Tenkai reestablished the shrine as Haruna-san Ganden-ji under Tendai Buddhism, governed by Kan'ei-ji Temple{{Sfn|群馬県教育委員会|1976|pp=6-7}}{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2012|pp=271-274}}. Numerous sub-temples and pilgrim groups (Haruna-kō) flourished{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2012|pp=274-275}}. === Modern period === In 1868, Shinto-Buddhist separation policies dismantled Buddhist elements{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2012|pp=585-593}}. Currently, the shrine is undergoing significant restoration projects totaling ¥2.3 billion from 2017 to 2025[https://web.archive.org/web/20170101135236/http://jomo-news.co.jp/ns/6914831942917445/news.html Haruna Shrine restoration]. == Haruna-kō == Haruna Shrine worship groups (Haruna-kō) spread across Kantō and surrounding regions during the Edo period{{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2012|pp=277-278}}{{Sfn|群馬県教育委員会|1976|pp=8-9,191}}{{Sfn|関|1999|p=48}}. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === Six shrine buildings designated on December 27, 2005{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|00003934|榛名神社}}: * Main shrine, offering hall, worship hall (1806) * Kokuso-sha and Gakuden (1725, 1814) * Kagura-den (1764) * Sōryū-mon (1856) * Shinkō-den (1859) * Zuishin-mon (1847) === Natural Monument === * "Yatate-sugi" cedar (Legend of Takeda Shingen’s arrow){{Sfn|榛名町誌編さん委員会|2012|p=605}} == Annual Festivals == (Various listed rituals and festivals) == Other Haruna Shrines == * {{ill|Haruna Shrine (Numata)|ja|榛名神社 (沼田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruna Shrine (Numata)|1=Haruna Shrine (Numata City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131275530}} * {{ill|Haruna Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|榛名神社 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruna Shrine (Shiroi)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Haruna Shrine (Hachiōji)|ja|榛名神社 (八王子市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haruna Shrine (Hachiōji)|1=Haruna Shrine (Hachioji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85881569}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Important Cultural Properties in Kantō#Gunma|ja|関東地方にある建造物の Important Cultural Properties一覧#群馬県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Important Cultural Properties in Kantō#Gunma|qq=}} == External Links == Prefecture]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated places of scenic beauty]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
